data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate plan-review + completion-verification onto the workflow engine; remove sample stubs) × THIS child 92fa (B-UX: Visual editor — select/create criteria, prompts & overlay triggers)]

Walked all 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster (B1 723c, B2 6b06, B3 dc58, B4 101a, B5 f12d, B6 7e48, B-RETIRE 961b, B-DM 5506, 0408). ONE high-confidence finding (conflicting assumptions / scope overlap with the PARENT). The other six modes read benign (footnoted). ANTI-FP: benign readings filtered; the one finding is reported because the parent's own What/Out-of-scope/Success/Acceptance criteria objectively never name an editor deliverable, a library WRITE path, or in-editor authoring.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FINDING 1 — Conflicting assumptions / scope overlap with the PARENT: B-UX is a child with no anchoring parent criterion.
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- finding: THIS child (B-UX visual editor) does not contribute, fully OR partially, to ANY of the parent epic's acceptance/success criteria, and its load-bearing assumption (the visual editor must gain library-backed pickers + in-editor criterion/prompt authoring, backed by a library WRITE path) is not an assumption the parent or any migration sibling shares. The parent's scope is exclusively (a) migrate plan-review onto the engine, (b) migrate completion-verification, (c) planned-trace parity, (d) cutover with signing intact, (e) remove throwaway samples — every one a READ/WIRE/CUTOVER operation over the EXISTING library. The parent's ONLY mention of "visual editing" is AC5/AC6's RETENTION of epic A's demo workflow "as the visual-editing refinement sample" (a sample to refine AGAINST, covered by B6) — not a commitment to deliver an editor upgrade or an authoring backend. The parent's What/Out-of-scope never names a visual editor, library pickers, in-editor authoring, or a create/update API. B-UX (and its backing data-model sibling B-DM 5506) therefore sits under this migration epic without an anchoring parent criterion.
- criteria: [parent What, parent Out of scope, parent Success Criterion, parent AC1, parent AC2, parent AC3, parent AC4, parent AC5, parent AC6]
- location: child 92fa Acceptance Criteria (all five) + Why/What/Scope; parent 72d5 What / Out of scope / Success Criteria / Acceptance Criteria
- evidence: ["Parent SC: 'plan-review and completion-verification run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate ... validated by offline planned-trace parity ...; only real workflows remain (samples removed)' — no editor / library-write / authoring deliverable.", "Parent What enumerates exactly 4 bullets (migrate plan-review, migrate completion-verifier, planned-trace parity, remove sample stubs) + 'KEEP epic A's demo workflow as the unused visual-editing refinement sample' — the sole visual-editing mention is RETENTION of a sample, not authoring of an editor or its backend.", "Child 92fa ACs are entirely about editor dropdowns sourced from the library, inline overlay-trigger create, in-editor criterion+prompt authoring via the B-DM write API, and a Playwright E2E — none maps to any parent AC.", "Child 92fa Why roots the justification in the migration's editing UX ('the editor must let the user SELECT criteria, prompts, and overlay triggers from lists and CREATE new ones'), but the parent never makes editor UX a deliverable.", "Whole-roster cross-check: every parent AC IS covered by a migration sibling — AC1->B2(6b06)/B1(723c); AC2->B3(dc58); AC3->B4(101a); AC4->B5(f12d)+0408; AC5/AC6->B6(7e48); SC = composite of B1/B2/B3/B4/B6. The migration/cleanup set is complete WITHOUT B-UX, so B-UX (and B-DM) are additive beyond the epic's bounded deliverables — i.e. no parent criterion is left UNCOVERED; this is child-without-parent-anchor, not a coverage gap.", "Backing sibling B-DM (5506) carries the SYMMETRIC G4 finding for the same lineage; B-UX is the front-end half. Resolving one (re-parent or add-parent-AC) should resolve both."]
- scenarios: ["Epic 72d5 reaches its AC6 end-state (two real workflows + one sample, lint+suite green) via B1/B2/B3/B4/B5/B6/0408 while B-UX (and B-DM) are still open -> the epic's AC/SC are fully satisfiable yet two children remain, so 'epic done' is ambiguous and the acceptance set does not bound the actual deliverables.", "A reviewer tracing parent criteria -> children finds B-UX with no parent anchor and cannot tell whether the epic's migration/cleanup goal depends on the editor work or is independent of it.", "Conversely, if the editor work is treated as in-scope, the epic can be blocked open on B-UX/B-DM even though all six stated ACs are met — completion is governed by criteria the AC set does not enumerate."]
- impact: Traceability / scope-broadening gap. B-UX carries net-new effort (front-end rework in editor_assets/src, a new editor-server endpoint, in-editor authoring form, real-browser Playwright E2E) plus a backing data-model sibling (B-DM), none of which any parent acceptance/success criterion accounts for. The epic can be 'done' per AC1–AC6 while B-UX/B-DM stay open (or vice-versa). No parent criterion is left uncovered (the migration set is fully covered by the other siblings), so this is a child-without-parent-anchor consistency finding, not a coverage gap.
- checklist_item: Check the 7 cross-child modes (implicit shared state, conflicting assumptions, dependency gap, scope overlap, ordering violation, consumer impact, residual references) — each detected mode is a finding.
- suggested_fix: Either (a) add a parent acceptance criterion making the editor + library write/enumerate data model an explicit epic deliverable (e.g. "the visual editor lets a human select/author the criteria + prompts of the migrated plan-review batch, backed by the library write/enumerate data model, drift-gate-green"), tying B-UX + B-DM to the epic's intent and stating whether AC6 'done' is gated on it; or (b) if the editor/library-write work is a separate concern (it depends on epic A's editor + the v3 batch schema, not the migration), re-parent B-UX (92fa) + B-DM (5506) out of this epic so 72d5's children are exactly the migration/parity/cleanup stories its ACs enumerate. If (a), also fix the within-lineage dual-write-path ambiguity already flagged on B-UX's G6 review (B-DM create/update vs the pre-existing prompt_authoring.save_prompt) so the lineage keeps one canonical drift-gate-green write path.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Modes read benign (no finding):

(1) Implicit shared state — B-UX writes user prompts/criteria via B-DM to .rebar/prompts; B2/B1 only READ packaged entries at render/run time. B-DM is designed NOT to dirty the committed reviewers/index.json (test_user_write_does_not_change_committed_index). No write-write race or stale-read contradiction across siblings. (A within-lineage dual-write-path concern — B-DM create/update vs the existing prompt_authoring.save_prompt — is folded into Finding 1's suggested_fix, not a separate sibling-level mode.) Benign.

(3) Dependency gap — B-UX declares depends-on B-DM (the library write/enumerate data model) and the v3 batch schema; B-DM (5506) blocks 92fa, so the producer/consumer link is present and ordered. B-DM left B-UX a precise hand-off comment (repo_root coherence for registry visibility; the {id,kind,title,description,inputs,outputs,is_overlay,execution_mode,category,source} field shape). No missing producer. Benign.

(4) Scope overlap — B-UX is the sole consumer of B-DM's enumerate/create API; B6 RETAINS examples/review_skeleton.yaml which B-UX CONSUMES as the visual-editing refinement target (retain-vs-consume, distinct ownership, not duplicated). No sibling duplicates B-UX's editor surface. Benign.

(5) Ordering violation — B-DM lands BEFORE B-UX ('per the epic's sequencing it lands BEFORE the UX'); B-UX correctly sits after the v3 batch schema (on main). Internally consistent. Benign.

(6) Consumer impact — B-UX is purely additive editor tooling; it does not change the live claim/close gate or any runtime gate path, so no consumer break at its boundary. The editor-server endpoint reuses the established loopback+token+Host-guarded /prompt/save security model (per the T3 review on this ticket). Benign.

(7) Residual references — B6 (7e48) removes only examples/code_review.yaml + templates.py, neither of which B-UX references; B-RETIRE (961b) removes the bespoke plan-review path but KEEPS passes.py/registry.py the library still uses. No sibling deletes or renames a symbol B-UX relies on. Benign.

ANTI-FP note: Finding 1 is reported as a single high-confidence finding because it is grounded in the parent's verbatim text — the parent's What/Out-of-scope/Success/Acceptance criteria objectively never name an editor, library pickers, in-editor authoring, or a write API, while every parent criterion is independently covered by a migration sibling; B-UX maps to none of them. It mirrors the symmetric G4 finding logged on backing sibling B-DM (5506) and the prior G3 structural finding on this same ticket. The remaining six modes are filtered out under the benign-reading rule.
